content.claim: Hungary's overall PISA performance (mathematics 473, reading 473, science 486) is close to OECD averages (472, 476, 485 respectively), but disadvantaged students reaching proficiency in basic skills (12.1%) lags below the EU average (16.3%)
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Outcome data does not attribute performance gaps to 6/8-year track mechanisms versus other factors (residential sorting, church-school exemptions, resource disparities)
Cross-sectional snapshot does not enable causal inference about tracking effects
Aggregate scores mask heterogeneity within student populations
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
